content: The black fox skin a shadow cast from the roof nigh to the floor; And sleek it seemed and soft it gleamed, and the woman stroked it o'er; And the man stood by with a brooding eye, and gnashed his teeth and swore.
style_prompt: Speaking at a standard pace, her energy level is low.

content: His cheeks were blanched as the flume head foam when the brown spring freshets flow; Deep in their dark, sin calcined pits were his sombre eyes aglow; They knew him far for the fitful man who spat forth blood on the snow.
style_prompt: The female speaks softly, with a moderate pace.

content: I was weary and sick and cold.
style_prompt: The girl speaks gently, avoiding haste and high energy.

content: "So that was the end of the great black fox, and here is the prize I've won; And now for a drink to cheer me up-I've mushed since the early sun; We'll drink a toast to the sorry ghost of the fox whose race is run."
style_prompt: Despite the low energy, her pitch remains elevated.

content: Wherefore it was beyond all laws that lusts of man restrain, A man drank deep and sank to sleep never to wake again; And the Yukon swallowed through a hole the cold corpse of the slain.
style_prompt: Speaking slowly and softly, her voice had an unexpectedly high pitch.

content: Ha!
style_prompt: Maintaining a regular volume, she deliberately speaks with a slow pace and higher pitch.

content: "The Moose hides called it the devil fox, and swore that no man could kill; That he who hunted it, soon or late, must surely suffer some ill; But I laughed at them and their old squaw tales. Ha!
style_prompt: Speaking at a regular tempo, her voice resonates with a noticeable treble.

content: "I tracked it up where the mountains hunch like the vertebrae of the world; I tracked it down to the death still pits where the avalanche is hurled; From the glooms to the sacerdotal snows, where the carded clouds are curled.
style_prompt: A woman's gentle voice is consistently high-pitched.

content: When thieves and thugs fall out and fight there's fell arrears to pay; And soon or late sin meets its fate, and so it fell one day That Claw fingered Kitty and Windy Ike fanged up like dogs at bay.
style_prompt: Discussing various topics, she maintains a high-pitched tone.

content: Grim shadows diapered the snow; the air was strangely mild; The valley's girth was dumb with mirth, the laughter of the wild; The still, sardonic laughter of an ogre o'er a child.
style_prompt: The woman's voice was tranquil and unhurried, her words softly spoken.

content: A bird sang for the joy of spring, so piercing sweet and frail; And blinding bright the land was dight in gay and glittering mail; And with a wondrous black fox skin a man slid down the trail.
style_prompt: A calm tone blends with her high-pitched voice.

content: Mad!
style_prompt: Speaking slowly and with fervor, the girl's tone remained consistently high and engaging.

content: For grub meant gold to our thinking, and all that could walk must pack; The sheep for the shambles stumbled, each with a load on its back; And even the swine were burdened, and grunted and squealed and rolled, And men went mad in the moment, huskily clamoring "Gold!"
style_prompt: Speaking with low energy, her high pitch stands out.

content: We built our boats and we launched them.
style_prompt: Talking with a treble, she maintains a natural, energetic flow.

content: Gold!
style_prompt: Her speech flowed unhurriedly, her voice maintaining a high pitch and energy in reserve.

content: We landed in wind swept Skagway.
style_prompt: The woman's words unfolded at a leisurely pace, her voice possessing both a high pitch and a quiet, low-energy quality.

content: Gold!
style_prompt: With a higher tone, she speaks slowly while keeping her energy normal.

content: Beneath us the green tumult churning, above us the cavernous gloom; Around us, swift twisting and turning, the black, sullen walls of a tomb. We spun like a chip in a mill race; our hearts hammered under the test; Then-oh, the relief on each chill face!--we soared into sunlight and rest.
style_prompt: With a deliberate speaking speed, she had a high-pitched tone and a quiet presence.

content: But what of the others that followed, losing their boats by the score? Well could we see them and hear them, strung down that desolate shore. What of the poor souls that perished?
style_prompt: With a lack of energy, her voice maintains a high pitch.

content: We roused Lake Marsh with a chorus, we drifted many a mile; There was the canyon before us-cave like its dark defile; The shores swept faster and faster; the river narrowed to wrath; Waters that hissed disaster reared upright in our path.
style_prompt: With subdued energy, her voice took on a sharp pitch as she spoke deliberately.

content: Spring! and the hillsides flourished, vivid in jewelled green; Spring! and our hearts' blood nourished envy and hatred and spleen. Little cared we for the Spring birth; much cared we to get on- Stake in the Great White Channel, stake ere the best be gone.
style_prompt: She softly murmured, her speech unfolding slowly, but her voice had a high key and low energy.

content: We joined the weltering mass, Clamoring over their outfits, waiting to climb the Pass. We tightened our girths and our pack straps; we linked on the Human Chain, Struggling up to the summit, where every step was a pain.
style_prompt: Speaking at a regular pace, she lacks high energy.

content: Gone was the joy of our faces, grim and haggard and pale; The heedless mirth of the shipboard was changed to the care of the trail. We flung ourselves in the struggle, packing our grub in relays, Step by step to the summit in the bale of the winter days.
style_prompt: Speaking softly, she keeps a normal pace of speech.

content: Thus toiled we, the army of fortune, in hunger and hope and despair, Till glacier, mountain and forest vanished, and, radiantly fair, There at our feet lay Lake Bennett, and down to its welcome we ran: The trail of the land was over, the trail of the water began.
style_prompt: In a hushed manner, she talks without high energy.

content: "Farewell!" we cried to our dearests; little we cared for their tears. "Farewell!" we cried to the humdrum and the yoke of the hireling years; Just like a pack of school boys, and the big crowd cheered us good bye. Never were hearts so uplifted, never were hopes so high.
style_prompt: Despite low energy, her voice had a high pitch.

content: Each man worked like a demon, as prow to rudder we raced; The winds of the Wild cried "Hurry!" the voice of the waters, "Haste!" We hated those driving before us; we dreaded those pressing behind; We cursed the slow current that bore us; we prayed to the God of the wind.
style_prompt: In a soft-spoken, high-pitched manner, she communicates.

content: "Klondike or bust!" rang the slogan; every man for his own. Oh, how we flogged the horses, staggering skin and bone! Oh, how we cursed their weakness, anguish they could not tell, Breaking their hearts in our passion, lashing them on till they fell!
style_prompt: Whispering gently, her voice maintains a high pitch.

content: Never was seen such an army, pitiful, futile, unfit; Never was seen such a spirit, manifold courage and grit. Never has been such a cohort under one banner unrolled As surged to the ragged edged Arctic, urged by the arch tempter-Gold.
style_prompt: With a tranquil rhythm, her words had a high pitch, energy subdued.
